oldContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Trust Based Mobility: Video Editing Specialist (Direct-Response / AI Video Editor) Anywhere in the World Wyoming Design Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://eddieron.com">https://eddieron.com</a> </p> <p>Trust Based Mobility is a performance-driven health and wellness brand that helps people move better, feel better, and regain confidence in their bodies. We produce high-volume direct-response video content for paid social and organic channels, and we're building a team of rockstar editors to help us ship 100+ video assets per week without sacrificing quality, speed, or storytelling.</p> <p>This is not a slow, academic editing role. It's a high-output creative role for a fast, organized, performance-minded editor who can turn raw ideas, scripts, AI-generated footage, voiceovers, hooks, and direct-response frameworks into ads that feel native, polished, and conversion-focused. You'll work closely with the Head of Performance Creative.</p> <p>Compensation is paid in USD: $1,500 to $3,000 per month base depending on experience, plus bonus potential up to $4,000 per month for A+ performance. This is a 1099 contractor, fully remote role. It begins with a 60-day paid trial, after which successful candidates become eligible for bonus compensation and paid time off. Our team runs on PST and we require at least 4 hours of daily overlap.</p> <p>What you'll do: edit and assemble performance-focused video ads for paid social and other growth channels; work from creative briefs, scripts, raw footage, AI-generated clips, voiceovers, and brand guidelines; build strong hooks, maintain pacing, and add accurate captions with no grammar mistakes; select music and sound effects, incorporate B-roll and AI-generated footage, and ensure every asset feels cohesive from hook to CTA; and maintain a clean editing workflow, meet deadlines, and communicate proactively.</p> <p>Required: fluent in at least one major editing workflow (Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut, or comparable); fluent, near-native English is mandatory, with accurate captions and comfort on team calls; AI-capable, able to build full AI-generated video ads using tools and models such as Veo 3, Kling 3.0, Seedance, and similar; comfortable creating voiceovers with tools like ElevenLabs and stitching AI scenes, voiceover, subtitles, graphics, and music into one harmonious edit; a solid understanding of direct-response video (hooks, pacing, captions, CTAs); e-commerce, direct-to-consumer, or digital product editing experience strongly preferred (not generic dropshipping content); and a clear, organized portfolio with playable video links.</p> <p>Nice to have: familiarity with project management tools like Notion, Monday.com, or ClickUp and Slack, plus an understanding of performance creative testing, Meta ads, TikTok-style pacing, and UGC structures. To apply, include a link to your portfolio with playable video links. Applications with broken links, unplayable files, or no portfolio will not be considered.</p> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/trust-based-mobility-video-editing-specialist-direct-response-ai-video-editor">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/trust-based-mobility-video-editing-specialist-direct-response-ai-video-editor</a></p> Thu, 23 Jul 2026 20:03:19 +0000 Sat, 22 Aug 2026 20:03:19 +0000 https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/trust-based-mobility-video-editing-specialist-direct-response-ai-video-editor https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/trust-based-mobility-video-editing-specialist-direct-response-ai-video-editor Nuuly: Nuuly Senior Software Engineer Anywhere in the World 🇺🇸 United States of America Pennsylvania Back-End Programming Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5983/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Philadelphia, PA <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.nuuly.com/">https://www.nuuly.com/</a> </p> <p></p> <p>Nuuly is hiring a Senior Software Engineer to join our Technology team. Building performant scalable cloud service environments is hard. Nuuly knows this firsthand, having built a cloud-based microservices platform from the ground up to power our online customer experience and drive the logistics and processing of our garment distribution centers. We're looking for a Senior Engineer with the initiative and expertise to help mature this platform into a stable, scalable, and robust backbone for launching the next wave of Nuuly innovations.</p> <p>You'll need strong experience designing and building microservices backed by relational and non-relational databases. Solid confidence with JVM-based tools such as Kotlin, Spring, and data persistence with Postgres and other database technologies. You’ll also need a reasonable amount of aptitude in designing and integrating Kubernetes services with cloud-based platform APIs such as Google Cloud Platform (GCP), AWS, or Azure focused on driving toward zero-downtime deployments.</p> <p>As a Senior Engineer, you'll bring experience that extends beyond personal code contribution: leading design for moderately complex features, driving communication between teams, providing critical feedback, coordinating with others, and providing mentorship as needed. You'll participate in incident response and post-mortem processes to improve system reliability. You should be adept at picking up new technologies and patterns and comfortable discussing and debating the pros and cons of various approaches. Most of all, you should be ready and willing to tackle new challenges with a supportive team of creative and hard-working professionals.</p> <p>Responsibilities:</p> <p></p> <ul> <li>Producing architectural proposals and engaging in discussions and decision-making for new features and platform evolution</li> <li>Collaborating with the product team to design solutions for stakeholder features and inform product decisions through technical feasibility analysis</li> <li>Mentoring junior engineers through code review, pair programming, and design discussions</li> <li>Delivering the whole lifecycle of deployable features: design, code, testing, configuration, documentation, deployment, and support</li> <li>Contributing to operational guidelines, system reliability practices, and post-mortem analysis for production incidents</li> <li>Teaching and enforcing development methodologies while proactively identifying improvement opportunities in codebase, tooling, and processes</li> <li>Collaborating with QA and Platform Engineering teams to test code and keep our integrated testing environments unblocked</li> <li>Providing technical guidance to cross-functional teams on integration approaches and system constraints to deliver features and evolve our architecture</li> </ul> <p>Qualifications:</p> <p></p> <ul> <li>5-7 years of software engineering experience with ~2 years of senior-level responsibilities such as taking ownership of designs or projects and working to coordinate work across teams</li> <li>Operational knowledge of one or more server-side object-oriented programming languages (Java, Kotlin, Scala, or other JVM languages preferred; Python, Go, Node/TypeScript also considered)</li> &l
newContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Abnormal: Campaign Program Manager Anywhere in the World Sales and Marketing Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote - USA </p> <h2><strong>About the Role</strong></h2> <p>We are looking for a highly organized, AI-forward Campaign Program Manager to drive the planning, orchestration, and standardization of integrated marketing campaigns. This is not a traditional program management role. This role serves as the operational hub between the Integrated Campaigns and functional marketing teams, ensuring every campaign has a comprehensive integrated marketing plan and is executed consistently across regions and channels. A strong understanding of campaign strategy is essential.</p> <p>The ideal candidate thrives at the intersection of strategy, process, execution, and AI-enabled productivity. They will partner closely with Campaign Managers to translate campaign strategies into executable demand plans, coordinate cross-functional execution, and create scalable campaign toolkits that empower regional and field marketing teams.</p> <p>This individual will be expected to leverage AI tools such as Claude and other emerging technologies to streamline planning, accelerate execution, improve collaboration, and scale campaigns to increase testing velocity, develop repeatable frameworks, and enable teams to move faster and operate more efficiently.</p> <h2><strong>What You'll Do</strong></h2> <h3><strong>Campaign Planning &amp; Execution</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Own and manage the integrated marketing calendar and demand plans across all segments, regions, and campaigns to ensure consistent coverage across the globe.&nbsp;</li> <li>Manage and maintain the global campaign messaging framework.</li> <li>Partner with Campaign Managers to develop and maintain a consistent integrated marketing plan for every campaign, including objectives, target audiences, messaging, timelines, KPIs, demand calendar, and execution requirements. Ensure all active campaign information is centralized, current, and easily accessible across the organization.</li> <li>Drive alignment across stakeholders and delivery teams, manage dependencies, and proactively resolve resource and execution risks across campaigns.&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate execution across Digital, Lifecycle, Events, SDR, Marketing Operations, Analytics, and Regional Marketing teams to ensure campaigns are live and in market</li> <li>Track campaign readiness, execution status, and performance while ensuring alignment to pipeline and go-to-market goals.</li> </ul> <h2>&nbsp;</h2> <h3><strong>Field &amp; Regional Marketing Enablement</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Develop campaign-in-a-box toolkits, playbooks, templates, and enablement resources that help regional teams execute campaigns consistently.</li> <li>Partner with Campaign Managers and Field Marketing to create scalable regional activation plans while allowing for local customization.</li> <li>Lead campaign enablement and training sessions and drive adoption of campaigns across regions.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>AI &amp; Operational Excellence</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Act as the AI champion for Integrated Marketing, identifying opportunities to apply AI across planning, execution, reporting, localization, and field enablement workflows.</li> <li>Build and maintain a library of prompts, workflows, templates, and AI playbooks that can be leveraged across Campaign, Field, and Functional Marketing teams.</li> <li>Develop AI-powered campaign planning frameworks that accelerate campaign creation, reduce time-to-launch, and improve execution consistency.</li> <li>Enable regional and field marketing teams to use AI to personalize campaigns, adapt messaging for local markets, and scale execution with limited resources.</li> <li>Partner with Marketing Operations and Analytics to explore AI-driven forecasting, campaign insights, and performance optimization.</li> <li>Drive a culture of rapid experimentation by leveraging AI to create, test, and optimize campaign messaging, channels, audiences, and creative variations at scale.</li> <li>Evaluate emerging AI tools and agents and recommend new capabilities that improve marketing productivity and effectiveness.</li> <li>Define and track AI adoption and productivity metrics across the marketing organization.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Management</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Manage completion of campaign scorecards, QBRs, and executive updates, - using AI to automate reporting where applicable</li> <li>Work with functional teams to gather channel-level campaign results&nbsp;</li> <li>Support Campaign Managers to facilitate post-campaign reviews and communicate learnings, priorities, and execution readiness across stakeholders.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What We're Looking For</strong></h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in campaign operations, integrated marketing, demand generation, program management, or project management.</li> <li>Strong understanding of B2B demand generation and integrated campaign execution.</li> <li>Proven success managing complex cross-functional initiatives and stakeholders.</li> <li>Experience developing campaign playbooks, enablement programs, or field marketing resources.</li> <li>Experience leveraging AI tools to improve marketing productivity, scale processes, and accelerate execution.</li> <li>Strong communication, organizational, and stakeholder management skills.</li> <li>Data-driven mindset with a passion for process improvement and experimentation.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-ML2</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Actual compensation will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors including skills, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.<br>In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for bonus or incentive compensation, equity, and a comprehensive benefits package.</p></div><div class="title">Base salary range:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$107,100</span><span class="divider">—</span><span>$154,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>AI and our hiring process</strong><br>Abnormal AI uses AI-assisted tools to help our recruiting team prepare for candidate interviews. These tools analyze resume content and role requirements to suggest interview questions and identify areas for the interviewer to explore. They do not make hiring decisions or screen candidates automatically. Every decision about a candidacy is made by a person.</p> <p><br>Abnormal AI is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law. For our EEO policy statement please&nbsp;<a href="https://assets.contentstack.io/v3/assets/blt3a076cfd9753fa1f/blt9cbda641db7c0045/6a1e3d3a66aea075cd33e462/AAP_and_EEO_St

Amazon Echo Show 21 The Echo Show 21 can act as the centerpiece of any burgeoning smart home. The 1080p smart display is a Matter and Thread controller, meaning you can locally control compatible devices using voice commands or a range of widgets. Its built-in auto-framing camera also facilitates video calls. Plus, it has decent speakers for music and other features shared among Alexa displays. Where to Buy: $399.99 $319.99 at Best Buy $399.99 $319.99 at Home Depot The Echo Show 21’s smart home controls are unique to it and the smaller Echo Show 15 that’s just $20 cheaper. The included voice remote can’t interact with widgets or Alexa skills, but you can use it to control your streaming services or other media. There’s a built-in 13-megapixel camera, too, in case you want to video chat with a pal, with auto-framing to keep you centered even if you’re rushing around the house. The panel is designed to mount on the wall, but if you’d rather set it on a counter or bookshelf, Best Buy is selling the adjustable stand for $99.99 (usually $124.99). Amazon , Best Buy , and Corsair are selling the Corsair Frame 4500X RS for $119.99, a $70 discount from the usual price. The versatile case supports motherboards up to EATX, GPUs up to 460mm in length, and has a seamless panoramic glass window that wraps around its front and left side. The case includes three fans with reverse rotors mounted to its right side, letting the ARGB lighting shine into the case while they pull in air. CORSAIR Frame 4500X RS Where to Buy: $189.99 $119.99 at Amazon $189.99 $119.99 at Best Buy $189.99 $119.99 at Corsair While the panoramic window might draw more attention, one of this case’s standout functional features is Corsair’s Infinirail system. Rather than having individual holes for fan screws, there are long slots on sliding rails, giving you more flexibility with cooling components and configuration. As a result, you can fit radiators up to 280mm or 360mm on the top or side of the case, depending on whether you opt for 120mm or 140mm case fans. The case also supports rear connection motherboards like this Gigabyte X870 Aorus for cleaner cable runs, and is ready for vertical GPU mounting if you buy a riser cable . There are some other nice inclusions as well, like an anti-sag GPU support arm, and the screws come in a reusable box rather than a series of tiny plastic bags. The case includes a two year manufacturer’s warranty. ]]> Andrew J. oldContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Abnormal: Campaign Program Manager Anywhere in the World Sales and Marketing Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote - USA </p> <h2><strong>About the Role</strong></h2> <p>We are looking for a highly organized, AI-forward Campaign Program Manager to drive the planning, orchestration, and standardization of integrated marketing campaigns. This is not a traditional program management role. This role serves as the operational hub between the Integrated Campaigns and functional marketing teams, ensuring every campaign has a comprehensive integrated marketing plan and is executed consistently across regions and channels. A strong understanding of campaign strategy is essential.</p> <p>The ideal candidate thrives at the intersection of strategy, process, execution, and AI-enabled productivity. They will partner closely with Campaign Managers to translate campaign strategies into executable demand plans, coordinate cross-functional execution, and create scalable campaign toolkits that empower regional and field marketing teams.</p> <p>This individual will be expected to leverage AI tools such as Claude and other emerging technologies to streamline planning, accelerate execution, improve collaboration, and scale campaigns to increase testing velocity, develop repeatable frameworks, and enable teams to move faster and operate more efficiently.</p> <h2><strong>What You'll Do</strong></h2> <h3><strong>Campaign Planning &amp; Execution</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Own and manage the integrated marketing calendar and demand plans across all segments, regions, and campaigns to ensure consistent coverage across the globe.&nbsp;</li> <li>Manage and maintain the global campaign messaging framework.</li> <li>Partner with Campaign Managers to develop and maintain a consistent integrated marketing plan for every campaign, including objectives, target audiences, messaging, timelines, KPIs, demand calendar, and execution requirements. Ensure all active campaign information is centralized, current, and easily accessible across the organization.</li> <li>Drive alignment across stakeholders and delivery teams, manage dependencies, and proactively resolve resource and execution risks across campaigns.&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate execution across Digital, Lifecycle, Events, SDR, Marketing Operations, Analytics, and Regional Marketing teams to ensure campaigns are live and in market</li> <li>Track campaign readiness, execution status, and performance while ensuring alignment to pipeline and go-to-market goals.</li> </ul> <h2>&nbsp;</h2> <h3><strong>Field &amp; Regional Marketing Enablement</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Develop campaign-in-a-box toolkits, playbooks, templates, and enablement resources that help regional teams execute campaigns consistently.</li> <li>Partner with Campaign Managers and Field Marketing to create scalable regional activation plans while allowing for local customization.</li> <li>Lead campaign enablement and training sessions and drive adoption of campaigns across regions.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>AI &amp; Operational Excellence</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Act as the AI champion for Integrated Marketing, identifying opportunities to apply AI across planning, execution, reporting, localization, and field enablement workflows.</li> <li>Build and maintain a library of prompts, workflows, templates, and AI playbooks that can be leveraged across Campaign, Field, and Functional Marketing teams.</li> <li>Develop AI-powered campaign planning frameworks that accelerate campaign creation, reduce time-to-launch, and improve execution consistency.</li> <li>Enable regional and field marketing teams to use AI to personalize campaigns, adapt messaging for local markets, and scale execution with limited resources.</li> <li>Partner with Marketing Operations and Analytics to explore AI-driven forecasting, campaign insights, and performance optimization.</li> <li>Drive a culture of rapid experimentation by leveraging AI to create, test, and optimize campaign messaging, channels, audiences, and creative variations at scale.</li> <li>Evaluate emerging AI tools and agents and recommend new capabilities that improve marketing productivity and effectiveness.</li> <li>Define and track AI adoption and productivity metrics across the marketing organization.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Management</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Manage completion of campaign scorecards, QBRs, and executive updates, - using AI to automate reporting where applicable</li> <li>Work with functional teams to gather channel-level campaign results&nbsp;</li> <li>Support Campaign Managers to facilitate post-campaign reviews and communicate learnings, priorities, and execution readiness across stakeholders.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What We're Looking For</strong></h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in campaign operations, integrated marketing, demand generation, program management, or project management.</li> <li>Strong understanding of B2B demand generation and integrated campaign execution.</li> <li>Proven success managing complex cross-functional initiatives and stakeholders.</li> <li>Experience developing campaign playbooks, enablement programs, or field marketing resources.</li> <li>Experience leveraging AI tools to improve marketing productivity, scale processes, and accelerate execution.</li> <li>Strong communication, organizational, and stakeholder management skills.</li> <li>Data-driven mindset with a passion for process improvement and experimentation.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-ML2</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Actual compensation will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors including skills, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.<br>In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for bonus or incentive compensation, equity, and a comprehensive benefits package.</p></div><div class="title">Base salary range:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$107,100</span><span class="divider">—</span><span>$154,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>AI and our hiring process</strong><br>Abnormal AI uses AI-assisted tools to help our recruiting team prepare for candidate interviews. These tools analyze resume content and role requirements to suggest interview questions and identify areas for the interviewer to explore. They do not make hiring decisions or screen candidates automatically. Every decision about a candidacy is made by a person.</p> <p><br>Abnormal AI is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law. For our EEO policy statement please&nbsp;<a href="https://assets.contentstack.io/v3/assets/blt3a076cfd9753fa1f/blt9cbda641db7c0045/6a1e3d3a66aea075cd33e462/AAP_and_EEO_St
newContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Dropbox: Senior Director, Revenue Operations Anywhere in the World Full-Stack Programming Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote - US: Select locations </p> <h2 class="p1"><span class="s1">Role Description</span></h2> <div><span class=" author-d-4z65zz66zl57z75zyiz66zfr2fz87zwz89znujgz72zurz70zuz72z610z65zz65zlez84zz89zz72z9lz84z1z87zz86zz86ziidbe">Dropbox is seeking a Head of Revenue Operations to lead and scale the operational foundation of the company's global B2B go-to-market organization. Dropbox to scale across products, regions, and sales motions, including product-led, sales-assisted, and enterprise.</span></div> <h2 class="p1"><span class="s1">Responsibilities</span></h2> <ul> <li> <div>Own and continuously improve Dropbox's end-to-end revenue operating model, including segmentation, account coverage, territory design, role clarity, and cross-functional handoffs across Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Support</div> </li> <li> <div>Lead annual and quarterly revenue planning processes, including bookings targets, quota setting, capacity planning, territory design, and ramp assumptions</div> </li> <li> <div>Establish forecasting rigor, pipeline management processes, inspection cadences, and performance management standards that improve visibility and predictability across the business</div> </li> <li> <div>Define, track, and operationalize core revenue and GTM KPIs (e.g., pipeline coverage, win rates, ACV, sales cycle, productivity, CAC, velocity, retention, and expansion), identifying performance gaps and driving operational improvements</div> </li> <li> <div>Lead revenue operations across the customer lifecycle, from lead routing and opportunity management through renewal and expansion, ensuring scalable processes, governance, and execution excellence</div> </li> <li> <div>Partner with Data Science on analytical support and insights, experimentation and cadence for biz performance updates</div> </li> <li> <div>Partner with Finance on revenue planning, pricing operations, discounting frameworks, deal governance, sales compensation, and incentive design to support growth and operational discipline</div> </li> <li> <div>Partner with Customer Success and Support leadership to improve retention, renewal, and expansion outcomes through operational insights, reporting, and process optimization</div> </li> <li> <div>Enable GTM readiness and operational execution for new product launches, packaging changes, pricing updates, and growth initiatives</div> </li> <li> <div>Deliver executive-level reporting, business reviews, and performance insights that support decision-making across revenue leadership</div> </li> <li> <div>Build, lead, and develop a high-performing Revenue Operations organization, driving operational excellence, scalable processes, and the adoption of AI-enabled solutions that improve productivity and business performance</div> </li> </ul> <h2 class="p1"><span class="s1">Requirements</span></h2> <ul> <li>12+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, GTM Operations, Finance, or related functions within a scaled SaaS or product-led growth company</li> <li>Proven experience leading revenue operations for multi-segment B2B organizations across SMB, Mid-Market, and Enterprise customer segments</li> <li>Deep expertise in forecasting, revenue planning, territory design, quota management, sales productivity, incentive compensation, and pipeline management</li> <li>Strong understanding of SaaS business models, revenue motions, and key operational drivers across acquisition, expansion, and retention</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to identify operational challenges and implement scalable, data-driven solutions that improve business performance</li> <li>Strong cross-functional leadership skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders across Sales, Finance, Product, Marketing, and Customer organizations</li> <li>Experience supporting organizations through significant growth and GTM evolution, including PLG, sales-assisted, and enterprise motions</li> <li>Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with extensive experience leveraging data to drive operational and business decisions</li> <li>Track record of building, developing, and leading high-performing revenue operations teams in a fast-paced, evolving environment</li> </ul> <h2>Preferred Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Proven track record implementing AI-enabled revenue solutions that deliver measurable improvements in efficiency, productivity, and business outcomes</li> <li>Demonstrated experience owning and scaling GTM technology ecosystems, with deep expertise in Salesforce and related revenue platforms</li> </ul> <h2><span class=" author-d-1gg9uz65z1iz85zgdz68zmqkz84zo2qowz81zz86z7z65z9wz73z7z75z6emz81zz70z4oz65zrz86z4dnz72ztz67zvoz67zl91">Durable Skills</span></h2> The case includes three fans with reverse rotors mounted to its right side, letting the ARGB lighting shine into the case while they pull in air. oldContent: Crunchbase News https://news.crunchbase.com/ Data-driven reporting on private markets, startups, founders, and investors Wed, 22 Jul 2026 18:24:30 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=6.8.6 https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/cb_news_favicon-150x150.png Crunchbase News https://news.crunchbase.com/ 32 32 The Rise And Rise Of Billion-Dollar-Plus Rounds https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/billion-dollar-plus-round-counts-rising-ai-fintech-healthcare-h1-2026/ Thu, 23 Jul 2026 11:00:53 +0000 https://news.crunchbase.com/?p=93868 Startup funding used to be associated with smallish bets on promising founders. But times change. While financings of a few million haven’t gone away, today most venture capital actually goes to rounds of a billion dollars or more. Moreover, it looks like a rising trend. So far this year, 60% of global startup funding across stages 1 — around $320 billion — went to rounds of $1 billion or more, per Crunchbase data. Such rounds were instrumental in pushing global funding for the first half of the year to record levels . The U.S. funding tallies are even more tilted to megadeals this year, with 73% of funding going to billion-dollar-plus rounds. Of the $290 billion invested in these deals, just two rounds for AI leaders OpenAI and Anthropic account for more than half the total. As you can see, the notion of billion-dollar-plus rounds accounted for a minority of funding before this year. The lone exception was the first quarter of 2025, when OpenAI closed a $40 billion financing. Not just bigger deals, more of them too Giant rounds aren’t just getting more ginormous. They’re happening with greater frequency too. So far this year, U.S. startups have closed 23 known rounds of $1 billion or more, per Crunchbase data. That puts 2026 already on par with 2025, a record-setting year, and we’ve still got about five months left. Not surprisingly, these megarounds are generally later-stage rounds or corporate financings. Only two of this year’s billion-dollar-plus rounds — Prometheus and World Labs — were seed or early-stage rounds, per Crunchbase data. Lessons from the first crop of billion-plus financings In the history of startups, meanwhile, the billion-dollar-plus venture funding round is a fairly contemporary phenomenon. The first American example, per Crunchbase data, was Uber ’s $1.2 billion Series D, in 2014. Over the next three years, a handful of others pulled in 10-figure rounds as well, including SpaceX , Airbnb , Lyft , SoFi , Snap , Grail , WeWork , Fanatics and Argo AI . Most of those companies went on to go public and reach valuations that well-exceeded levels set for prior megarounds. SpaceX ($1.6 trillion recent market cap), Uber ($148 billion) and Airbnb ($87 billion) were the standout success stories. Two of the megafund recipients — Argo AI and WeWork — did not fare so well, while a third, cancer diagnostics provider Grail, has been up and down. Fanatics, meanwhile, remained private and is still thriving. If these early billion-plus fundings taught investors anything, it was that pouring unusually large sums into well-regarded unicorns can be quite lucrative but is far from a sure bet. Uncharted territory In the current funding cycle, it’s not enough to ask whether billion-dollar rounds have potential for high returns. With Anthropic and OpenAI, the question now applies to rounds in the tens of billions or even over $100 billion. As both have already filed confidentially to go public, it may not take us long to find out. Related Crunchbase query: Billion-Dollar-Plus Startup Funding Rounds In 2026 Related reading: Crunchbase Data: Global Startup Investment Hit Record $510B In H1 2026 As AI Boom Accelerates Funding And Exits Crunchbase Data: Q2 Brought The Most Billion-Dollar Startup Exits Since 2021 The $100M+ Round Is Now Just Your Typical Late-Stage Financing Illustration: Dom Guzman Seed through growth-stage rounds for private companies founded in the past 20 years. ↩ ]]> https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/rise-of-massive-funding-blank-check.jpg Is On-Prem Making A Comeback? https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/on-prem-systems-vs-cloud-security-sagie/ Thu, 23 Jul 2026 11:00:09 +0000 https://news.crunchbase.com/?p=93864 A PBX vendor recently told me something I did not expect to hear: customers are asking for on-premise systems again. Looking broader into the entire market, I can see how this makes a lot of sense. Companies are becoming increasingly uneasy about where critical infrastructure and sensitive data live. AI fraud is getting better. Voice cloning is becoming more convincing. Vibe coding is allowing less experienced developers to build faster, but not always more securely. Quantum computing is still over the horizon, but serious companies are already thinking about what it may mean for encryption and long-term data protection. For the past decade, cloud migration was treated as the obvious strategy. It gave companies speed, scale and lower upfront costs. Startups could launch without buying servers. Enterprises could modernize without rebuilding their own infrastructure. That logic still holds. But we are witnessing an interesting shift where progress is happening so fast, security cannot keep up, thus creating an uneasy feeling causing decision-makers to revert back to older, and perhaps safer perceived strategies. Here are three trends that I believe are pushing on-prem back into the limelight. AI fraud is changing the security conversation In many cases, cloud providers are more secure than what a company could build internally. The issue is that thanks to AI, attackers are becoming more sophisticated, and quick. AI makes phishing more polished, fake invoices more believable, and voice impersonation harder to detect. A call that sounds like the CFO or CEO asking for a payment approval is no longer far-fetched. That changes how companies think about exposure. The attack surface is not only servers. It is identity systems, SaaS tools, APIs, employee workflows, permissions, contractors and support portals. For sensitive systems such as communications, payments, identity and customer data, control becomes more valuable. On-prem does not guarantee security. But it can reduce dependency on outside platforms and give companies clearer ownership over the systems they cannot afford to compromise. Enterprise AI may favor private infrastructure Cloud AI APIs are excellent for testing. A company can launch a pilot quickly without buying GPUs, managing models, or hiring a large infrastructure team. But enterprise AI is moving into production. That changes both the economics and the risk. The most useful enterprise AI applications require proprietary data: contracts, source code, customer records, financial reports, support tickets, security logs, medical files and internal communications. This is the data that gives AI business value. It is also the data companies are most careful with. For these use cases, on-prem or private AI infrastructure becomes more attractive. The model can run closer to the data. Access can be controlled more tightly. Retention, compliance and audit requirements become easier to manage. There is also a cost angle. Token pricing is convenient in a pilot, but expensive at scale. When thousands of employees or customers use AI every day, paying per query can become a serious recurring cost. For stable, high-volume workloads, owning or controlling the infrastructure may be cheaper than renting every interaction forever. Quantum risk is making long-term data protection more strategic Quantum computing is not breaking enterprise encryption today. But the risk is already part of serious security planning. The concern is that the minute quantum becomes commercial, all encrypted data sitting in the cloud will be transparent. No existing encryption will hold against a quantum computer. That matters most for companies holding long-life sensitive data: banks, healthcare prov
newContent: Crunchbase News https://news.crunchbase.com/ Data-driven reporting on private markets, startups, founders, and investors Thu, 23 Jul 2026 16:20:48 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=6.8.6 https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/cb_news_favicon-150x150.png Crunchbase News https://news.crunchbase.com/ 32 32 General Catalyst Takes The Lead Over Y Combinator In Backing $5M+ Fintech Deals https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/fintech-funder-general-catalyst-leads-deal-count-q2-2026/ Fri, 24 Jul 2026 11:00:46 +0000 https://news.crunchbase.com/?p=93874 For the first time in several quarters, General Catalyst in Q2 overtook Y Combinator when it came to participating in the most fintech deals of $5 million or more, per Crunchbase data. Notably, the quarter also marked the busiest one for General Catalyst since 2021 in terms of investing in rounds of $5 million or above. The firm’s next-busiest fintech investing quarter in rounds of that size was the fourth quarter of 2025, when it participated in 10 raises of $5 million or above. Overall, fintech startups raised $28.6 billion globally in the first half of 2026, a 22.7% increase from the first half of 2025, but down 17.3% compared to the $34.6 billion raised in the second half of last year. (It’s important to note that H2 2025 marked the strongest six-month funding period for fintech startups since the second half of 2022.) Over the past year, startup accelerator Y Combinator has routinely ranked as the most active investor in the fintech space. And overall, it was still the most active investor in the second quarter of this year, participating in 41 deals. But this time, it ranked behind General Catalyst in terms of backing fintech rounds in the $5 million or more category. General Catalyst participated in 12 of those deals, while YC and Index Ventures each invested in 11. In overall fintech dealmaking, General Catalyst still ranked far behind YC’s 41, with 13 deals. Coinbase Ventures participated in 12, Index Ventures in 11, and FJ Labs in 10. Top lead investors at $100M or more For megarounds — those deals of $100 million or more — we once again saw private equity firms topping the list of lead or co-lead investors. Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan , Iconiq Capital , GIC , Centerbridge Partners and Prosus topped that list, according to Crunchbase data. The largest rounds in Q2 were raised by a geographically diverse bunch of fintech startups. They include: Expense management startup Ramp was the fintech sector’s largest recipient of capital in the second quarter, raising a massive $750 million Series F round in June co-led by Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan, Iconiq Capital and GIC that valued the company at over $50 billion post-money. Ebury , a London-based cross-border payments and foreign-exchange fintech majority-owned by Santander , was a close second — landing $748 million in a private equity financing led by Centerbridge Partners in April. Also in April, Indian consumer lending startup KreditBee raised $220 million in a Series E round co-led by Dragon Fund , Hornbill Capital Advisers and Motilal Oswal Alternates that valued it at more than $1.5 billion. Paris-based insurtech Alan landed a $545 million Series G led by Prosus that valued it at $6.2 billion. Top fintech investors at seed When it comes to investing in seed rounds, unsurprisingly, Y Combinator again topped the list — by far, with 33 fintech deals. Next up was Rebel Fund with seven investments at the seed stage, and then Antler with six. The investor base shifted when we looked at who led or co-led post-seed rounds in the second quarter. General Catalyst topped that list, with five deals. TCV , SMBC Asia Rising Fund , Portage Ventures , Index Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners and Accel all tied with three investments each. Related Crunchbase query: Global Financial Services Venture Funding In 2026 Related reading: Fintech Funding Surges 23% In H1 2026 As Investors Concentrate Their Bets On AI And Financial Infrastructure Illustration: Dom Guzman ]]> https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/Money_Clip.jpg The Biggest AI Talent Challenge Is Resilience, Not Speed https://news.crunchbase.com/ai/biggest-talent-challenge-resilience-vaidya-crafting/ Fri, 24 Jul 2026 11:00:03 +0000 https://news.crunchbase.com/?p=93876 By Sumeet Vaidya Frontier labs and hyperscalers promise world-shifting innovation. And most deliver it. But, as we’re seeing with the Anthropic policy flip-flop and the evolving Hugging Face and OpenAI security incident , they operate without stability. That’s deeply concerning because technology organizations that build their entire AI operations and business on top of Anthropic, OpenAI and other paid models need to be able to depend on their reliability. Sumeet Vaidya Meanwhile, open-source organizations like OpenClaw and DeepSeek offer cost-free models with similar quality. The difference in price is stark. And the gaps in utility, safety and accessibility that kept the enterprise away are closing fast. This evolving dynamic leaves CTOs, CIOs and engineering leaders with a question: How can we keep reliability up and costs down when it’s impossible to predict whether hyperscalers will drop or raise prices of their next models? The answer isn’t clear-cut — yet. But it’s never been clearer that engineering leaders need systems that allow their teams to quickly swap models and shift how AI agents work with people and access real data and tools. Building the right foundational layer keeps organizations nimble enough to evolve alongside the industry without cutting corners by chasing the latest trends. Tokens cost more than time and money Engineering leaders at Big Tech companies and within enterprises learned the hard way that building toward their organization’s long-term stability is a much better plan than chasing trends like “tokenmaxxing,” which results in unsustainable spend and team burnout. While a fair amount of damage to company accounts and executive reputations has been done, the pendulum is already swinging back from tokenmaxxing to more sober approaches. At the same time, companies like Meta that publicly went all-in on team-wide AI use are shifting toward reinvesting in engineering team culture. The goal: boosting morale while removing competition from token use. Instead of jumping on the next hype train and creating the inevitable bottleneck, organizations should invest in modernizing their infrastructure to empower teams to sustainably iterate on and experiment with AI tools at scale. The future of enterprise AI empowers people and agents to work seamlessly together. What this looks like: Accepting that agents have most of the same capabilities as people, with the added value of being able to test against real infrastructure with access to “real” data swiftly and at scale. Ensuring agents have the same guardrails as teams, including making sure credentials and permissions are only granted when needed; under the right circumstances and with full visibility into actions taken when things go wrong. Building systems that are able to swap in the latest AI models and frameworks to take advantage of new advancements without losing the custom work done in-house. Making sure their companies aren’t locked into a single provider long-term in order to reduce risk from outages, expensive contracts or dated products. Models change. Update your architecture Building resilience starts with accepting that models and how we use them will change. Engineering leaders need to embrace that it will sometimes make sense to go with the latest hyperscaler model. Other times, it will make sense to bring in open-source models with novel harnesses that run at no cost but change how people collaborate with them. Meanwhile, agents shouldn’t be limited to toy problems or synthetic environments. They need the ability to test against real infrastructure, interact with realistic datasets, and participate meaningfully in rea

oldContent: The Verge The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Amazon Echo Show 21 The Echo Show 21 can act as the centerpiece of any burgeoning smart home. The 1080p smart display is a Matter and Thread controller, meaning you can locally control compatible devices using voice commands or a range of widgets. Its built-in auto-framing camera also facilitates video calls. Plus, it has decent speakers for music and other features shared among Alexa displays. Where to Buy: $399.99 $319.99 at Best Buy $399.99 $319.99 at Home Depot The Echo Show 21’s smart home controls are unique to it and the smaller Echo Show 15 that’s just $20 cheaper. They boast support for Zigbee, Matter, and Thread, providing seamless control for numerous devices. The included voice remote can’t interact with widgets or Alexa skills, but you can use it to control your streaming services or other media. There’s a built-in 13-megapixel camera, too, in case you want to video chat with a pal, with auto-framing to keep you centered even if you’re rushing around the house. The panel is designed to mount on the wall, but if you’d rather set it on a counter or bookshelf, Best Buy is selling the adjustable stand for $99.99 (usually $124.99). Amazon’s Echo Show 21 is all of those things in one, with a huge 21-inch screen. You can use it to control your smart lights, glance at recipes, watch TV shows, and more. Currently, Best Buy and Home Depot have the product on sale for $319.99 (usually $399.99), matching the lowest price we’ve seen to date. Amazon Echo Show 21 The Echo Show 21 can act as the centerpiece of any burgeoning smart home. The 1080p smart display is a Matter and Thread controller, meaning you can locally control compatible devices using voice commands or a range of widgets. Its built-in auto-framing camera also facilitates video calls. Plus, it has decent speakers for music and other features shared among Alexa displays. Where to Buy: $399.99 $319.99 at Best Buy $399.99 $319.99 at Home Depot The Echo Show 21’s smart home controls are unique to it and the smaller Echo Show 15 that’s just $20 cheaper. They boast support for Zigbee, Matter, and Thread, providing seamless control for numerous devices. The included voice remote can’t interact with widgets or Alexa skills, but you can use it to control your streaming services or other media. There’s a built-in 13-megapixel camera, too, in case you want to video chat with a pal, with auto-framing to keep you centered even if you’re rushing around the house. The panel is designed to mount on the wall, but if you’d rather set it on a counter or bookshelf, Best Buy is selling the adjustable stand for $99.99 (usually $124.99). In a notice published today , the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said that complaints about Tesla's mechanical door release don't warrant a defect investigation. Instead, the issue is better addressed through a broader rulemaking that would "mandate a robust and obvious door egress system in all motor vehicles," the safety regulator said. If finalized, all new vehicles would be subject to the new requirements. Multitaskers who want more memory in their next Chromebook should check out Best Buy , which is discounting Acer’s Chromebook Plus Spin 514 for the remainder of the day. Originally $749 for the configuration with 12GB of RAM, a MediaTek Kompanio Ultra 910 processor, and 256GB of UFS storage, it’s down to $559 right now. It’s a good price for what we consider to be one of the best Chromebooks you can currently buy. Acer Chromebook Plus Spin 514 (2025) Where to Buy: $749 $5599 at Best Buy My colleague Antonio G. Di Benedetto had a lot of nice things to say about this model in his October 2025 review. He praised the capabilities of its Arm-based processor, which is uncommon for a Chromebook review, as well as its day-plus battery life. The Spin 514 is great to use as a laptop, with a tactile keyboard and satisfying mechanical trackpad, but its 14-inch 1920 x 1200 120Hz IPS touchscreen can bend backwards to be used as a tablet. No stylus is included, but you can use any USI 2.0 stylus you’d like. If you want to know about other good discounted options, Lenovo’s Chromebook Plus 14 that Antonio likes a bit more is $649 (originally $999) at Best Buy . While it has the same processor and storage amount, it’s the model to buy instead if you want a little more RAM (16GB), an OLED screen, superior speakers, and fingerprint biometric support. The charger costs $55.99 (originally $79.99). Meanwhile, it’s $79.99 for Prime members at Amazon , but includes a USB-C cable whereas the Best Buy option doesn’t. One of the most notable traits of this charger — its downward facing ports that reduce its sag in the outlet — has become common since it launched early last year. The silver charger can deliver up to 140W through two of its USB-C ports when a single device is plugged in. It automatically splits the power depending on how many gadgets you have plugged in. If you’re a Sam’s Club member , or perhaps may soon become one, you can get $100 in digital Nintendo gift cards for $79.98. You can purchase up to five, which is enough to buy a bunch of games and accessories through Nintendo, or even a console. It’ll cost you $400 to get $500 worth of credit, meaning you can save on a Switch 2 console if you want to stack the savings to their maximum. The Switch 2 will increase in price starting in September , rising from $449.99 to $499.99, so if you’re on the fence, buying one sooner than later is a wise choice. Many of Logitech’s recent PC accessories can connect wirelessly via 2.4GHz to its Bolt receiver. Whether you lost your old one, or perhaps you want to upgrade to the newer USB-C version of the Bolt receiver that’s included with the MX Master 4 mouse, Amazon and Walmart have USB-C Bolt receivers available at just over half off. Originally $14.99, they’re $7.24, making it less expensive to grab a spare to connect your devices to. If you want to pair your device to a new Bolt receiver, you’ll need to do that through Logitech’s Options Plus app on desktop PC. ]]> Stevie Bonifield https://www.theverge.com/?p=970618 2026-07-24T11:00:03-04:00 2026-07-24T11:00:03-04:00 Boox's new Tile lineup will take on Xteink's popular e-readers that are small enough to stick on the back of an iPhone with MagSafe. As spotted by Good E-Reader , the first one is the Picco, which will feature a 3.97-inch front-lit, black-and-white e-paper display and expandable storage with an SD card slot. Boox confirmed to The Verge that the Picco is in the works, noting that it's part of a new Tile lineup and will have an "accessible price point," but without any other details about its plans, like specs, the specific price, or a release date. The front-lit display could put it on par with Xteink's new $99 X4 Pro e-reader , which The Ver … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Robert Hart https://www.theverge.com/?p=970534 2026-07-24T10:44:15-04:00 2026-07-24T10:43:55-04:00 Trump’s science adviser Michael Kratsios has no science background. | Image: Getty / The Verge The Trump administration unveiled the first "Genesis Mission" grants on Thursday, directing $5 billion toward hundreds of AI-driven science projects in an effort the White House has described as "comparable in urgency and ambition to the Manhattan Project." At roughly the same time, Trump's science adviser Michael Kratsios was on Capitol Hill selling lawmakers on an equally grandiose promise: " A New Golden Age " of American science, one that would prioritize artificial intelligence, robotics, and nuclear energy and downplay life sciences. The silver charger can deliver up to 140W through two of its USB-C ports when a single device is plugged in. It automatically splits the power depending on how many gadgets you have plugged in. If you’re a Sam’s Club member , or perhaps may soon become one, you can get $100 in digital Nintendo gift cards for $79.98. It’ll cost you $400 to get $500 worth of credit, meaning you can save on a Switch 2 console if you want to stack the savings to their maximum. The Switch 2 will increase in price starting in September , rising from $449.99 to $499.99, so if you’re on the fence, buying one sooner than later is a wise choice. Many of Logitech’s recent PC accessories can connect wirelessly via 2.4GHz to its Bolt receiver. Look no further than <a href="https://lemon.io/for-developers/?utm_source=weworkremotely&amp;utm_medium=job_ad&amp;utm_campaign=supply_en_vue_fullstack_jul_2026_job_ad">Lemon.io</a> — the marketplace that connects you with hand-picked startups in the US and Europe.</p> <p><strong>What we offer:</strong></p> <ul> <li>The rate depends on your seniority level, skills and experience. We've already paid out over $11M to our engineers.</li> <li>No more hunting for clients or negotiating rates — let us handle the business side of things so you can focus on what you do best.</li> <li>We'll manually find the best project for you according to your skills and preferences.</li> <li>Choose a schedule that works best for you. It’s possible to communicate async or minimally overlap within team working hours.</li> <li>We respect your seniority so you can expect no micromanagement or screen trackers.</li> <li>Communicate directly with the clients. Most of them have technical backgrounds. Sounds good, yeah?</li> <li>We will support you from the time you submit the application throughout all cooperation stages.</li> <li>Most of our projects involve working in a fast-paced startup environment. We hope you like it as much as we do.</li> <li>Through our community, we will connect you with the best developers from more than 75 countries.</li> </ul> <p>We have different positions for Senior Vue Developers, including front-end and full-stack roles. Please check the requirements for each option in the topic below.</p> <p>We also have many other positions available — please check the job listings below.</p> <h3><strong>Requirements for Senior Vue.js &amp; Nuxt.js Developer:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience</li> <li>4+ years of experience with Vue.js</li> <li>2+ years of experience with Nuxt.js &amp; JavaScript</li> <li>Experience with Typescript is a must</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements for Senior Vue.js &amp; PHP Developer:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience</li> <li>3+ years of experience with Vue.js &amp; 3+ years with PHP</li> <li>Experience with Laravel is a must</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements for Senior Vue.js &amp; Node.js Developer:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience</li> <li>3+ years of experience with Vue.js &amp; 3+ years with Node.js</li> <li>Experience with Nuxt.js is a must</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements for Senior Vue.js &amp; .NET Developer:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience</li> <li>3+ years of experience with Vue.js &amp; 5+ years with .NET<br>OR<br>5+ years of experience with Vue.js &amp; 3+ years with .NET</li> <li>Experience with Typescript is a must</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements for Senior Vue.js &amp; Python Developer:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience</li> <li>3+ years of experience with Vue.js 3+ years with Python</li> </ul> <p><strong>Other requirements:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong technical skills: as a Senior Developer, you are expected to be able to create projects from scratch and have a deep understanding of application architecture.</li> <li>Clear and effective communication in English — advanced ability to discuss business tasks, justify decisions, and communicate issues. Good self-presentation is also essential for upcoming client calls.</li> <li>Strong self-organizational skills — ability to work full-time remotely with no supervision.</li> <li>Reliability — we want to trust you and expect that you won’t let us and the client down.</li> <li>Adaptability and Flexibility — the ability to onboard the project promptly after accepting it and start delivering results quickly.</li> </ul> <p>Sounds good for you? Apply now and join the <a href="https://lemon.io/for-developers/?utm_source=weworkremotely&amp;utm_medium=job_ad&amp;utm_campaign=supply_en_vue_fullstack_jul_2026_job_ad">Lemon.io</a> community!</p> <p><strong>NOT YOUR TECH STACK?</strong></p> <p>We have a variety of projects, so if you have 4+ years of commercial software development experience and are proficient in AI Agent Architecture, AI Automation Architecture, AI Engineering, Site Reliability Engineering, Platform Engineering, React &amp; Python, React &amp; Golang, Golang, React &amp; Java, React &amp; Ruby, Ruby, React &amp; .NET, Android &amp; iOS, iOS &amp; Swift, Flutter &amp; Dart, Blockchain (Ethereum/Ethers.js/Wagmi/Viem/Solana), Angular &amp; Node.js, DevOps, MLOps, Data Science, Angular &amp; PHP, Angular &amp; .NET, Symfony &amp; React, Symfony &amp; Vue, Symfony &amp; Angular, Python, Symfony &amp; JavaScript &amp; Next.js &amp; TypeScript, Data Analysis, React &amp; PHP, Data Engineering, Project Management, Product Management, QA Automation, QA Manual, Embedded Software Engineering, React Native &amp; Node, React Native &amp; React, React Native &amp; Ruby, React Native &amp; Python, WordPress, Android, Data Annotation, Angular, Python &amp; Node, React &amp; Node.js, Java &amp; Spring, Svelte &am
newContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 OnTheGoSystems: Data Analyst – AI Translation Quality Anywhere in the World Nevada Data Analysis, AI Research, and Data Visualization All Other Remote Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/2344/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://onthegosystems.com">http://onthegosystems.com</a> </p> <p>At OnTheGoSystems, we're building AI systems that help people translate content across many languages. Our LLM team works with AI-generated translations, user edits, customer feedback, and quality data to continuously improve translation quality.</p> <p>We're looking for a Data Analyst who enjoys solving open-ended problems, finding patterns in complex datasets, and turning data into practical recommendations. This is not a reporting role. You'll decide what to measure, investigate why things happen, and help the team make better decisions based on evidence.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <p>You'll own analytical problems from start to finish.</p> <p>For example, you may discover that users edit translations much more often for one language pair than another. Instead of simply reporting it, you'll investigate why. You'll form hypotheses, gather the data, test possible explanations, and recommend the next steps.</p> <p>Your work will include:</p> <ul> <li> <p>Defining meaningful metrics for translation quality, AI performance, and user behavior.</p> </li> <li> <p>Monitoring trends and identifying unusual patterns or quality issues.</p> </li> <li> <p>Turning observations into structured investigations and testing multiple hypotheses.</p> </li> <li> <p>Extracting and combining data from SQL databases, NoSQL databases, logs, APIs, and CSV exports.</p> </li> <li> <p>Comparing results across language pairs, AI models, providers, customers, content types, traffic levels, and other dimensions.</p> </li> <li> <p>Separating real signals from coincidence by validating findings with data.</p> </li> <li> <p>Reviewing translation samples to confirm whether patterns represent genuine quality issues.</p> </li> <li> <p>Investigating root causes and recommending practical improvements.</p> </li> <li> <p>Evaluating multilingual AI output for meaning, terminology, tone, formality, grammar, pronouns, and consistency.</p> </li> <li> <p>Using AI tools, dictionaries, and other resources to investigate languages you don't speak, while recognizing when expert linguistic input is needed.</p> </li> <li> <p>Reviewing AI-generated analyses, identifying incorrect conclusions, and helping improve our automated evaluation systems.</p> </li> <li> <p>Building repeatable reports, scripts, and monitoring processes.</p> </li> <li> <p>Communicating findings clearly to developers, product managers, and linguists.</p> </li> </ul> <h2>What we're looking for</h2> <ul> <li> <p>Strong analytical thinking, curiosity, and attention to detail.</p> </li> <li> <p>The ability to work independently and define what should be investigated.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience turning open-ended questions into structured analyses and actionable recommendations.</p> </li> <li> <p>A solid understanding of metrics, segmentation, distributions, correlations, and the difference between correlation and causation.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience writing SQL, Python, or similar scripts to collect, clean, and analyze data.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience working with multiple data sources, including databases, logs, APIs, and exported datasets.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong hands-on experience using AI tools while critically evaluating their output.</p> </li> <li> <p>Fluent English plus at least one additional language (preferably European), with the ability to assess translation quality and explain language issues.</p> </li> <li> <p>Clear written communication and the ability to explain analytical findings to non-technical colleagues.</p> </li> </ul> <p>Bonus points for experience with:</p> <ul> <li> <p>Translation quality or localization.</p> </li> <li> <p>Multilingual content.</p> </li> <li> <p>Data visualization or business intelligence tools.</p> </li> </ul> <h2>Why join us?</h2> <p>You'll work on real AI systems used by customers around the world and directly influence how we measure and improve machine translation quality.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who enjoys exploring difficult questions, testing ideas, and discovering insights that aren't immediately obvious. You'll have the freedom to propose your own analyses and the responsibility to turn them into evidence-based recommendations.</p> <h2>What we offer</h2> <ul> <li> <p>100% remote position,</p> </li> <li> <p>Body and Mind Movement program to fill you up with energy,</p> </li> <li> <p>A computer budget to make your workplace better,</p> </li> <li> <p>A Kindle device with access to our company Amazon account,</p> </li> <li> <p>We respect national holidays in each country and want you to have a rest these days,</p> </li> <li> <p>Great opportunity to progress and advance,</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborating with team members across the globe.</p> </li> </ul> <p>If you're excited by data, languages, AI, and solving complex problems, apply now, and we'd love to hear from you!</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/onthegosystems-data-analyst-ai-translation-quality">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/onthegosystems-data-analyst-ai-translation-quality</a></p> Fri,
